characters.name: Aunt Mafalda
characters.description: Aunt MafaldaAunt Mafalda(Heather MacRae) –ClarissaandFerguson'saunt from Canada whom Clarissa dislikes and tries to get rid of in two episodes in the first two seasons. She first appears inSeason 1'sepisode "Haunted House".

characters.name: Clarissa Darling
characters.description: Clarissa Marie  Darling(Melissa Joan Hart) is the title character, Clarissa, who is a intelligent, sarcastic and realistic teenage girl. Despite her rationalism, she often tends to exaggerate any problem she's facing. She was approximately 14 years old when the series began and was a 9th grader atThomas Tupper Junior High. Her hobbies include computer game programming, photography, journalism and rock music. Though she is usually mild-mannered, she can be just as selfish and calculating as her younger brotherFerguson. Clarissa is best known for her unique fashion sense, typically involving very colorful, mismatched clothing. During in Season 1, Clarissa had an alligator named Elvis. Elvis was removed from the show after the first season. In the first episode of season two Clarissa explained that her parents sent Elvis back to Florida when he got too big, Mitchell Kriegman's novel, Things I Can't Explain, Clarissa further elaborated that he "disappeared under mysterious circumstances" and "it took years to get mom to confess that she gave away the reptilian Elvis without telling me after Ferguson tried to shove a pocket watch down his throat thinking it would win him the role of Captain Hook in the school play." Clarissa is a highly intelligent free spirit who often clashes with authority and order, usually represented by her family. She's known to program her own computer games to show off to her best friend and next-door neighbor Sam, whose friendship she deeply values. Outspoken and often bold, Clarissa usually follows her moral compass, but she also isn't above some sibling rivalry or petty drama. She's unique, she's sharp, and she's a 90's American teenage girl.

characters.name: Clifford Spleenhurfer
characters.description: Clifford SpleenhurferClifford Spleenhurfer(David Eck) – Originally a bully who harassed Ferguson in Season 1, he developed a crush onClarissawhen she stood up to him. He first appears inSeason 1'sepisode "Bully". She later misses his affections inSeason 2, and they begin a relationship which ends inSeason 4. He appeared in a total of five episodes, the most of any recurring character. His defining characteristic was a big appetite, much to Marshall's annoyance.

characters.name: Debbie Anders
characters.description: Debbie AndersDeborah "Debbie" Anders(Susan Greenhill) isSam'sestranged mother who is often on the road with a traveling women's Roller Derby team. She appears in athird-seasonepisode "Sam's Swan Song", where she tries to take custody of Sam and make him move to Seattle. She appears again in thefourth seasonepisode "Tale of Two Moms", when she stays with the Darlings and becomes a burden.

characters.name: Dr. Festerspoon
characters.description: Dr. FesterspoonDr. Festerspoon(Bob Noble) – The family doctor, who appears in two episodes inseasons 1and4.

characters.name: Elise Quackenbush
characters.description: Elise Quackenbush(portayed by Cassidy Rae) is an American teenage girl who appeared inSeason Two'sepisode "Sam in Love". In the episode, Clarissa feels left out whenSamcancels plans with her to spend time with Elise, his new girlfriend.

characters.name: The Soapersteins
characters.description: The Soapersteinsare the Darlings' mostly unseen next-door neighbors, referred to continually throughout the series. The mother and daughter appear once in season 4, and the family's dog appears inseason 5.

characters.name: Eve Biderberg
characters.description: Eve Biderberg(Russell Koplin) is an American teenage girl who appears in episode(s) of the show. She is first seen in thefourth seasonepisode "Hero Worship", where she is new girl at school, who annoys Clarissa when she begins copying her style.

characters.name: Ferguson Darling
characters.description: Ferguson W. Darling(Jason Zimbler) isClarissa'syounger brother, a mischievous redhead. He and Clarissa continually antagonize each other. She refers to him with several epithets such as "Ferg-face", "Fergwad" or "Fergbreath." He loves money and comes up with get-rich-quick schemes. He is also a Young Republican who idolizes Dan Quayle and Ronald Reagan. He goes to school with her and is about two years younger than her. Despite their rivalry, they occasionally collaborate, usually to the advantage of both. Unlike Clarissa, however, he does not seem to be very popular at school. Jason Zimbler identified strongly with his character. He told Mashable that he's also "unrepentantly nerdy and proud of learning." He liked that Ferguson "made being bookish cool - or cool for being uncool."

characters.name: Hillary O'Keefe
characters.description: Hillary O'Keefe(Sara Burkhardt) isClarissa'sfriend fromThomas Tupper Junior High, who appears in four episodes in seasons 2 and 3. Appears in:

characters.name: Janet Darling
characters.description: Janet Darling(Elizabeth Hess) –ClarissaandFerguson'smother. She is the only family member Clarissa sees as a voice of reason and thus she seeks advice from her occasionally. She is a teacher who works at a children's museum. She is also an environmentalist and an organic food enthusiast who often cooks bizarre meals.

characters.name: Janet Darling
characters.description: Janet Darling(Elizabeth Hess) –ClarissaandFerguson'smother. She is the only family member Clarissa sees as a voice of reason and thus she seeks advice from her occasionally. She is a teacher who works at a children's museum. She is also an environmentalist and an organic food enthusiast who often cooks bizarre meals.

characters.name: Marshall Darling
characters.description: Marshall Darling(Joe O'Connor) –ClarissaandFerguson'sfather. He is an architect who designs unusually shaped buildings, mostly retail and tourist attractions (such as the "Fryfel Tower"). Clarissa sometimes comes to him for advice, but he is less reliable in this role than Janet. He often calls Clarissa "sport", seldom addressing her by name. He and Janet are former flower children from the 1960s.

characters.name: Mrs. Cheesebrow
characters.description: Mrs. Cheesebrowis the school guidance counselor atThomas Tupper Junior High. She councilsClarissaduring the time of thesecond seasonand tries to get Clarissa interested in "normal" activities in "Misguidance Counselor".

characters.name: Olivia DuPris
characters.description: Olivia DuPrisOlivia DuPris(Nicole Leach) – Another one ofClarissa'sfriends from high school, who appears in seasons 4 and 5.

characters.name: Piper
characters.description: Piper is a human

characters.name: Sam Anders
characters.description: Samuel "Sam" Anders(Sean O'Neal) –Clarissa'sbest friend and confidant. ##Generally more optimistic and upbeat than she is, he often asks "What's the worst that can happen?" He is perhaps best known for entering her second-story bedroom with a ladder after a distinctive guitar chord plays followed by Clarissa's "Hi, Sam" greeting. Creator Mitchell Kriegman explained that Sam entering the house through Clarissa's bedroom window was "a way to get him into her room [...] and start interacting faster" as well as keeping their relationship strictly platonic. He is smart and polite and enjoys skateboarding. His parents are divorced (mentioned in thepilot) and it is mentioned early on that his mother left his father to join a Roller Derby. Sam's father works as a sports journalist and later gives Clarissa the opportunity to complete some work experience with him.
